findings.evidence: `IsUnipotent` is defined in the same namespace as `AffineAlgGroup`; Mathlib grep on `class Is.*: Prop extends` returns 10+ examples of the convention.
findings.fix: Rename to `IsAffineAlgGroup` to match the `Is`-prefix convention used by every other prop-class in this file and throughout Mathlib.
findings.issue: `AffineAlgGroup : Prop` lacks the `Is`-prefix that Mathlib uses for `Prop` classes (cf. `IsSimpleGroup`, `IsAlgClosed`, `IsAbelianGalois`, `IsRealClosed`, `IsNoetherian`); the same file already uses `IsUnipotent` correctly, so the bare form is also internally inconsistent.

findings.evidence: Same convention evidence as the `AffineAlgGroup` finding; `IsSimpleGroup` in `Mathlib/GroupTheory/Subgroup/Simple.lean:38` is the closest analogue.
findings.issue: `ReductiveGroup : Prop` (and `SemisimpleGroup : Prop` on line 68) drop the `Is`-prefix that Mathlib applies to prop-classes (cf. `IsSimpleGroup`, `IsAlgClosed`, `IsAbelianGalois`).

findings.evidence: Body is `h.unipotent_radical_trivial`; class field is `unipotent_radical_trivial : True` (line 65).
findings.fix: Delete the lemma and rely on dot-notation access (`h.unipotent_radical_trivial`); if a wrapper is later needed, name it after the actual content the unipotent radical provides, not after the act of rephrasing a placeholder.
findings.issue: `ReductiveGroup.unipotentRadical_trivial'` is a `True`-valued rephrasing of the class field `unipotent_radical_trivial`; the `'` suffix names the proof act (rephrasing) rather than a conclusion, and the body is a one-token projection of a `True` field.

findings.evidence: Field declaration: `unipotent_radical_trivial : True`; same overstatement applies to `radical_trivial : True` on line 70.
findings.fix: When the unipotent radical is actually defined (Layer 5), give the field a content-bearing type (e.g. `R_u (A ⊗[k] k̄) = ⊥`). Until then, either drop the placeholder or use a name that signals it is a stub (e.g. suffixed `_placeholder`).
findings.issue: Field `unipotent_radical_trivial : True` advertises a real mathematical claim (the unipotent radical over `k̄` is trivial) in its name, but the proposition is `True`, so no content is asserted — a name-strength-vs-statement-strength overstatement.

findings.evidence: Roadmap README, Layer 0: "R-points as a group. Generalize `AlgPoints R A := A →ₐ[k] R` for every commutative `k`-algebra `R`".
findings.fix: Either drop `AlgPoints` (it has no consumers in this PR) or define it now with the general signature from the roadmap, e.g. `def AlgPoints (R A : Type*) [CommSemiring R] [CommRing A] [Algebra k R] [Algebra k A] := A →ₐ[k] R`.
findings.issue: `AlgPoints (k A) := A →ₐ[k] k` reserves the name `AlgPoints` for a k-specialized definition, but the roadmap's Layer 0 plan uses `AlgPoints R A := A →ₐ[k] R` for the general functor of points; the same name with two different signatures will force a rename later.

summary: Three `Prop` classes inconsistently skip the `Is`-prefix that Mathlib uses for prop-classes (`IsSimpleGroup`, `IsAlgClosed`, `IsAbelianGalois`, etc.), a class field is `True` and its rephrased lemma is named after the proof act, and `AlgPoints` reserves the roadmap's general functor-of-points name with a k-specialized signature.
